engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Scudo box Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Scudo box in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Scudo box RHK engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - causes loss of power and engine management light from 60k miles.
DPF regeneration failure - blocked particulate filter on low-mileage urban vans.
Fuel injector wear - rough idle and misfires on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: Fiat Scudo box 9HU engine - diesel 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF clogging on short journeys - common failure on urban delivery vans under 50k miles.
Turbocharger actuator failure - loss of boost and limp mode from 70k miles.
In-tank fuel pump wear - hard starting and power loss on high-mileage examples.

cards.altText: Fiat Scudo box AH01 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: AdBlue/SCR system faults - NOx sensor failures and dosing pump issues on Euro 6 units.
High-pressure fuel pump wear - power loss and rattling noise on early-production examples.
EGR cooler leaks - coolant loss and overheating risk on high-mileage vans.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £3,200-£6,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 2.0 Multijet units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Scudo box Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Scudo box across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 2.0 HDI for Scudo box, 118bhp output with robust DW10 architecture but early EGR/DPF issues.
compatibleVariants: Box 2.0 HDI 118, Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI 118, Combi 2.0 HDI 118
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging from 50k miles causing power loss and P0490 codes
DPF regeneration failure on short-journey urban vans
In-tank fuel pump wear leading to hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHK Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: High-output 134bhp DW10 variant, most common Scudo box engine but DPF/EGR prone on low-mileage use.
compatibleVariants: Box 2.0 HDI 134, Box 2.0 Multijet 4x4, Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI 134, Combi 2.0 HDI 134
commonFailures: Differential pressure sensor failure (P0472/P0470) triggering limp mode
Turbo actuator sticking causing boost loss and P0299 codes
Swirl flap mechanism wear on pre-2011 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHR Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 5 update with 120bhp, improved emissions but retained DW10 DPF vulnerability on urban routes.
compatibleVariants: Box 2.0 HDI 120, Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI 120, Combi 2.0 HDI 120, Crew Cab 2.0 HDI 120
commonFailures: EGR cooler leaks causing coolant loss and overheating risk
DPF clogging on vans under 30k miles with frequent stop-start use
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing rattling noise from 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHH Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: 128bhp Euro 5 facelift engine with revised ECU mapping, common on 2011+ Scudo box variants.
compatibleVariants: Box 2.0 HDI 128, Box 2.0 Multijet 4x4, Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI 128, Combi 2.0 HDI 128
commonFailures: Anti-pollution fault codes (P1457) from DPF sensor failures
Fuel injector seal leaks causing diesel smell and rough idle
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring preventative replacement at 120k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for RH02 Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Urban-focused 90bhp diesel for light-duty Scudo box, DPF issues common on low-mileage delivery use.
compatibleVariants: Box 1.6 HDI 90, Chassis Cab 1.6 HDI 90, Combi 1.6 HDI 90
commonFailures: DPF clogging from short journeys causing P1457 and limp mode
Turbocharger actuator failure leading to boost loss and P0299
In-tank fuel pump wear causing hard starting and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HU Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Revised DV6 with updated ECU for 2011+ Scudo box, marginal DPF improvement but same core vulnerabilities.
commonFailures: EGR valve sticking causing rough idle and P0490 codes
DPF pressure sensor circuit faults (P0472) triggering depollution warnings
Oil pump wear on high-mileage units causing low oil pressure warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HM Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Latest 180bhp Multijet with AdBlue/SCR for Euro 6, complex emissions system but strong performance for heavy loads.
commonFailures: AdBlue dosing pump failure causing NOx sensor errors and limp mode
High-pressure fuel pump wear on early-production units causing rattling
EGR cooler leaks leading to coolant loss and overheating risk
entries.cta: Get quotes for AH01 Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Rare petrol option for Scudo box, 140bhp naturally aspirated unit with good reliability but limited UK availability.
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure causing misfires and rough idle from 60k miles
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring replacement at 100k miles
Throttle body carbon buildup causing hesitation on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for RFH Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Base petrol engine for early Scudo box, 79bhp naturally aspirated unit with simple design but low power for commercial use.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption from 70k miles
Water pump failure leading to overheating if service intervals missed
Alternator bearing wear causing charging system warnings on high-mileage vans
entries.cta: Get quotes for 220 A2.000 Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: First-generation 16v HDI for 220 series Scudo box, robust mechanical design but pre-DPF emissions technology.
compatibleVariants: Box 2.0 JTD 16v, Chassis Cab 2.0 JTD 16v, Combi 2.0 JTD 16v
commonFailures: Swirl flap mechanism failure causing inlet manifold damage on pre-2006 units
Turbocharger oil seal leaks leading to blue smoke and oil consumption
Injector seal leaks causing diesel smell and rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHW Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: 8v JTD engines for early Scudo box, simple mechanical design with good longevity but lower power output.
compatibleVariants: Box 2.0 JTD 8v 94bhp, Box 2.0 JTD 8v 109bhp, Chassis Cab 2.0 JTD 8v
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure requiring preventative replacement at 80k miles
EGR valve clogging causing power loss and black smoke on high-mileage units
Fuel pump wear leading to hard starting and power loss from 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHX/RHZ Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Scudo box Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Scudo box's 2.0 HDI and 1.6 HDI diesel engines dominate UK replacement demand, with EGR/DPF failures and fuel system issues most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your van.

problems.group: Fiat Scudo box EGR Valve Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Scudo box EGR Valve Failure - Why 2.0 HDI Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Scudo box Box 2.0 HDI 120/128/134, Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI, Combi 2.0 HDI (2007-2016, RHK/RHR/RHH/RH02)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve clogs with carbon deposits, restricting airflow and triggering engine management faults.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or blank EGR valve, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may return if DPF also clogged; not MOT-compliant if blanked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned EGR valve with updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 20k-40k miles if DPF system healthy; addresses root cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.0 HDI with upgraded EGR cooler and DPF
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime solution; includes 12-month warranty; best for vans over £4k value

problems.recommendation: On a Fiat Scudo box worth under £4,000 with over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components often offers better long-term value than repeated repairs. For lower-mileage vans, EGR replacement may suffice if the DPF is still functional.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt RHK/RHR Fiat Scudo box engine prices

problems.h4: Fiat Scudo box DPF Clogging - Why Urban Vans Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Scudo box Box 1.6 HDI 90, Box 2.0 HDI 120/128/134 (2007-2016, 9HU/9HM/RHH/RH02)
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filter cannot regenerate on frequent stop-start urban routes, leading to ash buildup and blockage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset and forced DPF regeneration cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if blockage is light; may fail again on same usage pattern

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace DPF unit with new sensors
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-60k miles if driving pattern improves; costly if EGR also faulty

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new or deleted DPF system
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage vans; eliminates recurring DPF costs

problems.recommendation: If your Scudo box is used primarily for urban deliveries under 20 miles per trip, a rebuilt engine with DPF delete (where legally permitted for off-road use) or upgraded filter may prevent repeat failures. For mixed-use vans, DPF replacement combined with EGR service offers a balanced solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Scudo box DPF replacement engines

problems.group: Fiat Scudo box Fuel Pump Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Scudo box Fuel Pump Failure - Why Engines Cut Out
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Scudo box Box 1.6/2.0 HDI all variants (2007-2016, 9HU/9HM/RHK/RHR)
problems.rootCause: In-tank fuel pump or high-pressure pump wear reduces fuel delivery, causing hard starting, power loss or stalling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace fuel filter, check relay and wiring
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses symptoms; won't fix worn pump internals

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new in-tank or high-pressure fuel pump with seals
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-80k miles if fuel quality maintained; labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded fuel system components
repairOptions.longevity: Best for vans over £3,500 value; eliminates fuel system worries

problems.recommendation: Fuel pump failures often indicate broader fuel system wear. On Scudo box vans over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded pumps and injectors typically offers better reliability and value than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 9HU/RHK Fiat Scudo box engine prices

problems.group: Fiat Scudo box P0472/P0470 Sensor Faults
problems.h4: Fiat Scudo box P0472/P0470 Sensor Faults - Why Limp Mode Activates
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Scudo box Box 2.0 HDI 128/134, 4x4 variants (2011-2016, RH02/RHR)
problems.rootCause: Differential pressure sensor circuit faults misread DPF status, triggering false depollution warnings and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace differential pressure sensor and reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix if DPF is healthy; sensor may fail again if wiring corroded

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose wiring harness, connectors and ECU communication
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses electrical root cause; may require specialist diagnostics

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated sensor package
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vans; includes warranty against sensor-related failures

problems.recommendation: P0472 faults often accompany DPF or EGR issues. If your Scudo box has over 90k miles and multiple fault codes, a rebuilt engine with updated sensors offers a comprehensive solution with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for RH02 Fiat Scudo box engine replacement

problems.h4: Fiat Scudo box Turbocharger Failure - Why Power Disappears
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Scudo box Box 1.6/2.0 HDI all turbo variants (2007-2016, 9HU/RHK/RHR/RH02)
problems.rootCause: Turbo actuator sticking or bearing wear reduces boost pressure, causing power loss, smoke and P0299 codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo actuator arm and reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Works if turbo internals are sound; may not fix bearing wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuild or replace turbocharger with new seals and bearings
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-70k miles if oil changes maintained; labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo system
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for vans over £4k; eliminates turbo and related component risks

problems.recommendation: Turbo failures often signal broader engine wear. On Scudo box vans approaching 100k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo and oil system components typically offers better long-term reliability than turbo-only repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt turbo diesel Fiat Scudo box engine prices

problems.group: Fiat Scudo box Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Scudo box Timing Belt Failure - Why Prevention Matters
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Scudo box all 1.6/2.0 HDI variants (2007-2016, 9HU/9HM/RHK/RHR/RHH/RH02)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt wear or tensioner failure causes belt slip or breakage, leading to valve/piston collision and catastrophic engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers and water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure; recommended at 100k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Repair bent valves, replace head gasket if belt slipped
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if damage is minor; often not cost-effective

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution after belt failure; includes warranty and upgraded parts

problems.recommendation: Timing belt replacement at 100k miles is essential preventative maintenance. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine is almost always more economical than attempting cylinder head repairs on a Scudo box.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Scudo box timing belt replacement engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Scudo box
finalCta.paragraph: EGR, DPF, fuel pump and turbo failures are common on Fiat Scudo box vans, but you don't need to accept main dealer prices or scrap your vehicle. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to address these known weaknesses. Compare quotes today and get back on the road with a 12-month warranty protecting your investment.

Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. engineSizes.h2: Fiat Scudo box Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Scudo box is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

groups.title: Fiat Scudo box Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Fiat Scudo box 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDI diesel was the entry-level engine for the Fiat Scudo box from 2007, offering 90bhp for urban delivery use but prone to DPF clogging on short-journey routes. This DV6-family engine powered base Box and Chassis Cab variants before being phased out in favour of the more robust 2.0L units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Scudo box 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Scudo box 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDI Multijet is the backbone of the Fiat Scudo box range, with outputs from 118bhp to 180bhp across DW10 and 507-series families. These engines power most Box, Chassis Cab and Combi variants but are known for EGR/DPF issues on low-mileage commercial use.
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Scudo box 2.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Fiat Scudo box Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Fiat Scudo box 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L petrol engine was a rare option on early Fiat Scudo box models, offering 79bhp naturally aspirated performance for light-duty use but limited UK availability and lower torque than diesel alternatives.
commonFailurePoints: alternator bearing wear on high mileage
items.cta: Compare Fiat Scudo box 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Scudo box 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L 16v petrol engine provided 140bhp for Scudo box variants requiring petrol power, with good reliability but higher fuel consumption than diesel equivalents and limited UK market presence.

engineYears.h2: Fiat Scudo box Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Scudo box production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The all-new PSA 270 platform Scudo box launched with shared architecture with Citroen Dispatch and Peugeot Expert, introducing modern HDI diesel engines.
keyChanges: New PSA 270 platform replacing outgoing 220 series
Introduction of 1.6L DV6 and 2.0L DW10 HDI diesel engines
DPF and EGR systems standard for Euro 4 compliance
Box, Chassis Cab and Combi body styles available
popularModels: Fiat Scudo box Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI
knownFor: Strong commercial payload capacity for light van segment
Early DPF clogging issues on urban delivery use
EGR valve failures triggering engine management lights
Cross-compatibility with Dispatch/Expert engines builds trust

years.ticker: Fiat Scudo box 2.0 HDI engine replacement • Scudo box EGR valve failure • DPF clogging Scudo box
enquiries: Fiat Scudo box 2.0 HDI engine replacement

years.description: Euro 5 emissions standards brought revised engine mappings and new codes like RHH and RH02, with marginal DPF improvements but persistent urban-use vulnerabilities.
keyChanges: Introduction of RHH 120bhp and RH02 128bhp engine codes
Revised ECU mapping for improved emissions compliance
Minor exterior facelift with updated grille and lights
knownFor: Marginal DPF improvement but same core vulnerabilities on short journeys
Differential pressure sensor faults (P0472) becoming common
EGR cooler leaks causing coolant loss on high-mileage units
4x4 variant gains popularity for rural commercial use
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011 Fiat Scudo box replacement engines
years.ticker: Scudo box P0472 code • RH02 engine replacement • Scudo box 4x4 engine

years.description: Final year of the PSA 270-series Scudo box before platform transition, with mature engine designs but increasing replacement demand on high-mileage commercial vans.
keyChanges: Last production year for PSA 270 platform Scudo box
No major engine changes; focus on run-out stock
Increasing replacement enquiries as vans reach 100k+ miles
Strong used market for Box and Chassis Cab variants
popularModels: Fiat Scudo box Chassis Cab 2.0 HDI 128
knownFor: High replacement demand as commercial vans exceed warranty
EGR/DPF failures common on vans used for urban deliveries
Strong parts availability due to PSA platform sharing
Cost-effective rebuilt engines extend van life profitably
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016 Fiat Scudo box replacement engines

years.description: All-new 507 series Scudo box arrives with Euro 6 compliance, AdBlue/SCR systems and the powerful AH01 180bhp Multijet engine for heavy-duty commercial use.
keyChanges: New 507 series platform with updated styling and tech
AdBlue/SCR emissions system standard for Euro 6
Enhanced connectivity and driver assistance features
popularModels: Fiat Scudo box Chassis Cab 2.0 Multijet 180
knownFor: Strong performance for heavy payloads and towing
AdBlue dosing pump and NOx sensor failures on early units
Higher replacement costs due to complex emissions systems
Limited rebuilt engine availability as model is still new

faq.h2: Fiat Scudo box Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Does a Citroen Dispatch or Peugeot Expert engine fit my Fiat Scudo box?
items.answer: Yes, the Fiat Scudo box shares its PSA 270/272 platform with Citroen Dispatch and Peugeot Expert, so most 1.6L and 2.0L HDI engines are directly interchangeable between these models.
keyPoints: RHK, RHR, RHH, RH02 codes fit all three PSA platform vans
ECU coding may be required after engine swap
DPF and EGR components must match your van's emissions spec
Always verify engine code via VIN before ordering replacement
items.cta: Compare Citroen Dispatch Peugeot Expert engine prices for Scudo box

items.question: How much does a Fiat Scudo box engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Fiat Scudo box engines typically cost £1,500-£4,800 supply-only depending on engine code, with 1.6L HDI at the lower end and 2.0L Multijet 180 at the premium end.
keyPoints: 1.6L HDI rebuilt: £1,500-£3,800 supply only
2.0L HDI rebuilt: £1,800-£4,500 supply only
Supply & fit adds £450-£950 labour depending on workshop
Prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from network specialists
items.cta: Get a tailored Fiat Scudo box engine replacement quote today

items.question: What are the most common Fiat Scudo box engine problems?
items.answer: EGR valve clogging, DPF regeneration failure and fuel pump wear are the top three issues driving Fiat Scudo box engine replacement enquiries in the UK.
keyPoints: EGR faults trigger P0490 codes and power loss from 50k miles
DPF clogging common on vans doing under 30-mile urban journeys
In-tank fuel pump wear causes hard starting and stalling
All three issues addressed in rebuilt engines with upgraded components
items.cta: Find rebuilt Fiat Scudo box engines with EGR/DPF upgrades

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Fiat Scudo box or should I scrap it?
items.answer: If your Scudo box is worth over £3,000 and the body/chassis is sound, a rebuilt engine at £1,800-£4,500 typically offers better value than scrapping and buying another van.
keyPoints: Commercial vans retain value if body and chassis are good
Rebuilt engine includes warranty protecting your investment
Supply & fit service minimises downtime for business use
Compare quotes before deciding - many vans are worth saving
items.cta: Compare Fiat Scudo box engine replacement vs van replacement costs

items.question: How long does a Fiat Scudo box engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most UK specialists can supply a rebuilt Fiat Scudo box engine within 1-3 days, with fitting taking 1-2 days depending on workshop availability and engine complexity.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on common engine codes like RHK/RHR
Fitting time varies: 1.6L HDI ~1 day, 2.0L Multijet ~1.5 days
Supply & fit packages coordinate delivery and fitting to minimise downtime
Book in advance during peak commercial vehicle repair periods
items.cta: Find Fiat Scudo box engine supply & fit with fast turnaround

items.question: What oil should I use in my Fiat Scudo box 2.0 HDI engine?
items.answer: Fiat specifies 5W-30 C3 low-ash oil for Scudo box 2.0 HDI engines to protect the DPF and EGR system; using incorrect oil accelerates component failure.
keyPoints: C3 specification oil essential for DPF longevity
Change interval: 12,500 miles or 12 months whichever comes first
Low-mileage urban vans may benefit from more frequent changes
Rebuilt engines often include first service oil and filter
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Scudo box engines with service packages

items.question: Do Fiat Scudo box engines have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: All Fiat Scudo box 1.6L and 2.0L HDI engines use a timing belt that must be replaced every 100,000 miles or 5 years to prevent catastrophic engine damage.
keyPoints: Timing belt failure causes valve/piston collision and engine destruction
Replacement cost £350-£650 at specialist vs £550-£950 at main dealer
Always replace water pump and tensioner at same time
Rebuilt engines include new timing kit as standard
items.cta: Compare Fiat Scudo box engines with new timing components fitted

items.question: Can I upgrade my Fiat Scudo box engine to a more powerful version?
items.answer: Yes, many Scudo box owners upgrade from 118bhp RHK to 134bhp RHR or 128bhp RH02, but ECU remapping and DPF compatibility must be verified before swapping.
keyPoints: Physical fitment is identical across DW10 family engines
ECU software must match engine code for emissions compliance
DPF and EGR components may need upgrading for higher output
Specialist advice recommended before undertaking engine swap
items.cta: Enquire about Fiat Scudo box engine upgrade compatibility

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Fiat Scudo box engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt Fiat Scudo box engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at the fitting workshop.
keyPoints: Warranty valid at any UK workshop in the specialist network
Covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Does not cover damage from incorrect installation or maintenance
Extended warranty options available for fleet customers
items.cta: Find Fiat Scudo box rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: How do I find my Fiat Scudo box engine code?
items.answer: Your Scudo box engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox flange and listed on the VIN plate under the bonnet or driver's door sill.
keyPoints: Common codes: RHK, RHR, RHH, RH02 for 2.0L; 9HU, 9HM for 1.6L
Enter your registration on EngineMarket to auto-identify your engine
Engine code determines compatible rebuilt units and pricing
Incorrect code selection leads to ECU compatibility issues
items.cta: Enter your reg to instantly identify your Fiat Scudo box engine code
